close_reason: Positive observation: ENV_LOCK pattern is correct
description:
src/lib.rs:421-424 declares 'static ENV_LOCK: Mutex<()> = Mutex::new(());' and the test put_with_pinned_version_returns_invalid_uri (lines 564-592) uses it correctly: lock acquired before set_var, SAFETY comment justifies the unsafe block, lock held until after remove_var.

The comment block at lines 421-424 explains exactly why the lock is necessary (from_uri_missing_token reads the env var; this test sets it; concurrent tokio tests would otherwise race).

A future contributor might:
  - Add a new test that touches GCP_ACCESS_TOKEN and forgets ENV_LOCK -> intermittent test failures.
  - 'Clean up' by removing the unsafe block on set_var when not needed (it became unsafe in Rust 1.83).
  - Replace std::sync::Mutex with tokio::sync::Mutex 'because async tests' (this would correctly fix the priority-2 'await holding lock' clippy finding, but must keep the locking discipline).

Defense: the ENV_LOCK pattern is correct. The SAFETY comments are correct. Future test additions touching GCP_ACCESS_TOKEN must follow this pattern.

NOTE: there is a separate priority-2 idiomatic-rust finding about the std::sync::Mutex held across .await in this test. Resolving that finding does NOT mean removing ENV_LOCK; it means switching to tokio::sync::Mutex or restructuring the test to drop the guard before the await.
title: Defend: ENV_LOCK + unsafe set_var pattern with SAFETY comments in tests
